_note: Grimoire & Steel character rules. This is the game's OWN design (not verbatim 1978): classes and races are kept separate. Restriction / special / prime-requisite text is authored for this game (adapted from classic rules) and is meant to be edited freely.

abilities.name: Strength
abilities.description: Physical power; affects melee attack/damage and feats of force. Prime requisite of Fighters.

abilities.name: Intelligence
abilities.description: Reasoning and memory; governs known languages and arcane spellcasting. Prime requisite of Magic-Users.

abilities.name: Wisdom
abilities.description: Willpower and intuition; governs divine spellcasting and resistance to mind effects. Prime requisite of Clerics.

abilities.name: Dexterity
abilities.description: Agility and reflexes; affects armor class, missile attacks and initiative. Prime requisite of Thieves.

abilities.name: Constitution
abilities.description: Health and stamina; affects hit points per hit die.

abilities.name: Charisma
abilities.description: Force of personality; affects reactions and the number/loyalty of hirelings.

alignments.name: Lawful Good
alignments.description: Acts with honor and compassion, upholding order for the benefit of all.

alignments.name: Chaotic Good
alignments.description: Values freedom and kindness, doing good in its own unconstrained way.

alignments.name: Lawful Evil
alignments.description: Pursues selfish ends through order, hierarchy and rules.

alignments.name: Chaotic Evil
alignments.description: Driven by destruction and self-interest with no regard for order or others.

alignments.name: Neutral
alignments.description: Avoids extremes, acting from self-interest, balance or indifference to grand causes.

races.name: Human
races.restrictions: None — humans may take any class and advance without level limits.
races.special_ability: Versatile and ambitious; may earn a prime-requisite experience bonus in any class and adapt to any culture.
races.description: The most numerous and adaptable of the playable peoples.

races.name: Elf
races.restrictions: Cannot use the very largest weapons comfortably; favors finesse over brute force.
races.special_ability: Infravision 60 ft; detects secret/hidden doors more readily; immune to ghoul paralysis; resistant to charm and sleep magic.
races.description: Long-lived, graceful folk at home in forests and twilight.

races.name: Half-elf
races.restrictions: Belongs fully to neither human nor elven society.
races.special_ability: Infravision 60 ft; improved chance to detect secret doors; partial elven resistance to charm and sleep.
races.description: Born of human and elf, balancing ambition with elven grace.

races.name: Halfling
races.restrictions: Small size; must use weapons cut down to size and cannot use the largest arms.
races.special_ability: +1 to all missile attacks; very hard to see in natural surroundings (hide outdoors); bonus to saving throws vs. magic; infravision 30 ft.
races.description: Small, nimble homebodies, braver than their size suggests.

races.name: Orc
races.restrictions: Suffers -1 to attack rolls in full daylight; distrusted by most civilized folk.
races.special_ability: Infravision 60 ft; strong and aggressive; functions well underground and at night.
races.description: Brutal, war-like cave-dwellers organized into raiding tribes.

races.name: Half-Orc
races.restrictions: Mistrusted by humans and orcs alike.
races.special_ability: Infravision 60 ft; hardy and strong, blending human adaptability with orcish toughness.
races.description: Born of human and orc, tough survivors caught between two worlds.

races.name: Dwarf
races.restrictions: Cannot use the largest weapons; must use arms suited to their stature.
races.special_ability: Infravision 60 ft; detects slanting passages, traps, shifting walls and new construction underground; bonus to saving throws vs. magic; resists poison.
races.description: Stout, stubborn mountain folk and master miners and smiths.

classes.name: Fighter
classes.restrictions: None notable — may use any weapon and any armor, including shields. Cannot cast spells.
classes.special_abilities: Best attack progression and hit dice of any class; gains multiple attacks against weak foes at higher level; may use any magical weapon or armor.
classes.description: A trained warrior whose strength of arms is unmatched.

classes.name: Magic-User
classes.restrictions: May not wear any armor or use shields, and may carry only a dagger as a weapon. Must memorize spells from a spellbook.
classes.special_abilities: Casts arcane spells of great power; may use most magic items such as wands, staves and scrolls.
classes.description: A studious wizard wielding memorized arcane magic.

classes.name: Cleric
classes.restrictions: May use only blunt (non-edged) weapons such as the mace or staff; may not use swords or bows.
classes.special_abilities: Casts divine spells granted by faith; can turn (and at high level destroy) undead; may wear any armor.
classes.description: A holy warrior-priest channeling divine power.

classes.name: Thief
classes.restrictions: May wear only leather armor and may not use shields.
classes.special_abilities: Backstab for extra damage from surprise; pick locks, find and remove traps, move silently, hide in shadows, climb sheer surfaces, pick pockets and hear noise.
classes.description: A stealthy rogue who relies on skill, cunning and surprise.

classes.name: Ranger
classes.restrictions: As Fighter; traditionally keeps few possessions and works alone or in small bands.
classes.special_abilities: All Fighter abilities, plus expert tracking, wilderness survival and bonus damage against giant-class and evil humanoid foes.
classes.description: A wilderness warrior and hunter of monstrous foes.

classes.name: Paladin
classes.restrictions: As Fighter; must be Lawful Good and uphold a strict code of honor or lose paladin powers.
classes.special_abilities: All Fighter abilities, plus detect evil, lay on hands to heal, immunity to disease and an aura that protects against evil.
classes.description: A holy knight sworn to righteousness and the protection of the innocent.

classes.name: Illusionist
classes.restrictions: As Magic-User: no armor or shields, dagger only.
classes.special_abilities: Specializes in illusion and phantasm magic — deceiving the senses and the mind.
classes.description: A magic-user devoted to the arts of illusion.

classes.name: Witch
classes.restrictions: As Magic-User: no armor or shields, dagger only.
classes.special_abilities: Wields curses and charms and brews magical potions; draws power from pacts and old lore.
classes.description: A practitioner of curses, charms and potion-craft.

classes.name: Druid
classes.restrictions: May use only natural or blunt weapons and only leather armor with a wooden shield; will not use metal armor.
classes.special_abilities: Casts nature spells; identifies plants, animals and pure water; at higher level can shapechange into animals.
classes.description: A priest of nature drawing power from the wild.

classes.name: Monk
classes.restrictions: May not wear armor and is limited to simple weapons.
classes.special_abilities: Martial-arts strikes that improve with level; an armor-class bonus while unarmored; a stunning blow and unusually fast movement.
classes.description: A disciplined ascetic who fights with body and will.

classes.name: Assassin
classes.restrictions: As Thief (leather only, no shields); must be of an evil alignment.
classes.special_abilities: All Thief abilities, plus assassination (a deadly strike from surprise), disguise and the use of poisons.
classes.description: A killer-for-hire blending thievery with lethal precision.

character_sheets.normal_items: Chain mail armor; shield; sword; short bow; quiver of 20 arrows; 1 silver arrow; 50' rope; 10' pole; 12 iron spikes; 6 torches; 1 week's standard rations; large sack; 1 quart of wine; wine skin
character_sheets.other_notes: Sample character from the D&D Basic character record sheet (used as the design guide).
